content.page_title: Best Registered Dietitians in London, Ontario (2026)
content.meta_description: Find top registered dietitians in London, Ontario for weight loss & nutrition counselling. Local experts, OHIP coverage info & pricing guide.
content.h1: Best Registered Dietitians in London, Ontario
content.intro: Looking for a registered dietitian in London, Ontario to help you lose weight and build healthier eating habits? We've researched the top local nutrition experts who can guide you through personalized meal planning, behaviour change, and sustainable weight management—whether you're just starting or need professional support.
content.local_context: London, Ontario is home to over 400,000 residents and has seen a rise in weight-related health concerns. The city has excellent access to registered dietitians through Western University's health programs, multiple private clinics, and OHIP-covered services through family doctors and the London Heal

top_picks.name: Nutrition Plus London
top_picks.tagline: Personalized nutrition counselling for lasting weight loss
top_picks.description: Nutrition Plus London is a private practice run by two registered dietitian nutritionists with over 15 years of combined experience in weight management and metabolic health. They offer one-on-one consultations, meal planning, and ongoing support for sustainable lifestyle change.
pros: Detailed food journals and tracking support
Strong focus on behaviour change, not just diet
cons: Not covered by OHIP; private pay only
top_picks.best_for: People seeking comprehensive, personalized nutrition coaching and willing to invest in private care

top_picks.name: London Health Sciences Centre – Nutrition Services
top_picks.tagline: Hospital-based dietitian services with OHIP coverage
top_picks.description: The nutrition department at London Health Sciences Centre offers registered dietitian consultations for weight management, diabetes prevention, and metabolic conditions. Referrals are typically made by your family physician and most services are covered by OHIP.
pros: Fully covered by OHIP with valid referral
Access to multidisciplinary weight loss team
top_picks.best_for: Residents with OHIP coverage seeking hospital-level care or those with complex medical conditions requiring coordinated nutrition support

top_picks.name: Ideal Protein London Clinic
top_picks.tagline: Medically supervised weight loss with proprietary meal replacement system
top_picks.description: Ideal Protein is a structured weight loss program combining meal replacements, coaching, and ongoing monitoring. Their London clinic employs registered health professionals and offers a proven approach to rapid, sustainable weight loss with weekly check-ins.
services_offered: Weekly weigh-ins and body composition analysis
cons: Less focus on long-term habit change after program
top_picks.best_for: People who prefer structured programs with clear guidelines and want rapid initial weight loss with professional oversight

top_picks.name: Wharton Medical Clinic – London Location
top_picks.tagline: Comprehensive weight loss clinic with physician and dietitian team
top_picks.description: Wharton Medical operates a full-service weight loss clinic in London combining physician assessment, registered dietitian nutrition counselling, and evidence-based treatment options. They offer both traditional nutrition support and medical interventions when appropriate.
pros: Access to medical interventions if needed
top_picks.best_for: Those seeking integrated medical and nutritional support, or individuals with complex health conditions requiring physician oversight

top_picks.name: Herbal Magic London
top_picks.tagline: Natural weight loss with nutrition coaching and herbal supplements
top_picks.description: Herbal Magic offers a natural approach to weight loss combining personalized nutrition plans, herbal products, and one-on-one coaching. Their London location has helped hundreds of residents achieve sustainable weight loss through plant-based nutrition strategies.
pros: Natural, plant-based approach appeals to many
cons: Herbal products add to monthly cost
Less emphasis on medical nutrition therapy
top_picks.best_for: Health-conscious individuals preferring natural approaches and willing to invest in herbal supplement programs

top_picks.name: Western University – Student Nutrition Clinic
top_picks.tagline: Affordable dietitian services provided by graduate students under supervision
top_picks.description: Western University's nutrition clinic offers low-cost consultations provided by registered dietitian students under the supervision of experienced registered dietitian nutritionists. An excellent option for budget-conscious Londoners seeking quality nutrition advice.
cons: May have longer sessions due to learning environment
top_picks.best_for: Budget-conscious residents and those who don't mind working with supervised graduate students for cost savings

what_to_look_for: Credentials: Ensure the provider is a registered dietitian (RD) with credentials from the College of Dietitians of Ontario (CDO) or similar regulatory body—not just a 'nutritionist'
OHIP coverage: Ask if services are covered by Ontario Health Insurance Plan; many require a physician referral but offer free or low-cost consultations
Approach to weight loss: Look for evidence-based methods focused on sustainable habit change, not fad diets or extreme restrictions
Follow-up support: Choose clinics that offer ongoing accountability through follow-up appointments, check-ins, or group support to maintain motivation
Specialization: If you have diabetes, heart disease, or other conditions, seek a dietitian with specific training in medical nutrition therapy
Accessibility: Consider location, parking, hours, and whether virtual consultations are available if in-person visits are difficult
content.average_cost_context: In London, Ontario, private registered dietitian consultations typically range from $80–$250 per session. OHIP-covered services through hospitals or physician referrals are free or low-cost. Structured weight loss programs with meal replacements run $180–$350 monthly.
content.insurance_notes: Ontario Health Insurance Plan (OHIP) covers registered dietitian services when referred by a physician for medical conditions like diabetes, heart disease, or obesity-related complications. Coverage is typically provided through hospitals or community health centres. Private insurance plans may cover a portion of fees; check your policy. Services at private clinics are usually out-of-pocket unless your employer offers extended health benefits.

faqs.question: Do I need a doctor's referral to see a dietitian in London?
faqs.answer: For OHIP-covered services through hospitals or public clinics, yes—you'll need a referral from your family physician. Private dietitian clinics in London do not require referrals; you can book directly. Check with your insurance provider, as some extended health plans may require referrals for reimbursement.

faqs.question: How much does a registered dietitian cost in London, Ontario?
faqs.answer: Private consultations range from $80–$250 per session depending on the clinic and whether it's an initial assessment or follow-up. OHIP-covered services are free with a valid referral. Structured programs (like Ideal Protein or Herbal Magic) cost $180–$350 monthly and include ongoing support.

faqs.question: What's the difference between a registered dietitian and a nutritionist?
faqs.answer: A registered dietitian (RD) is a regulated healthcare professional with standardized credentials, university training, and oversight by the College of Dietitians of Ontario. 'Nutritionist' is not a protected title in Ontario and anyone can use it. Always verify credentials with the CDO to ensure you're seeing a regulated professional.

faqs.question: How long does it take to see results working with a dietitian in London?
faqs.answer: Most people notice initial changes within 2–4 weeks (energy, digestion, cravings). Meaningful weight loss typically becomes visible in 6–8 weeks with consistent effort. A good dietitian focuses on sustainable change rather than rapid weight loss, which is more likely to last long-term.

faqs.question: Can I get virtual nutrition consultations with London dietitians?
faqs.answer: Yes, many London clinics now offer virtual appointments via video call, especially private practices like Nutrition Plus London and Herbal Magic. Ask when you book. Hospital-based services may have limited virtual options; check with LHSC directly.

faqs.question: Is weight loss covered by OHIP in Ontario?
faqs.answer: OHIP covers nutrition counselling for weight-related medical conditions (obesity, diabetes, heart disease) when referred by a physician. Preventive weight loss counselling for otherwise healthy people is typically not covered. Private insurance may offer partial coverage; review your plan details.

faqs.question: How do I choose between a private dietitian and a hospital-based clinic in London?
faqs.answer: Choose private if you want flexibility, personalized service, and faster appointments. Choose hospital-based if you have OHIP coverage, complex medical conditions, or prefer multidisciplinary support. Many Londoners use both—OHIP services for medical oversight and private sessions for personalized coaching.

content.cta_heading: Ready to find your ideal dietitian in London?
content.cta_body: Sign up for our weekly nutrition and weight loss tips for Canadians, plus updates on the best local health resources in your area. We'll help you stay motivated and informed every step of the way.
